segments.text: Keep silence before Me, O coastlands, And let the people renew their strength! Let them come near, then let them speak; Let us come near together for judgment.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who raised up one from the east? Who in righteousness called him to His feet? Who gave the nations before him, And made him rule over kings? Who gave them as the dust to his sword, As driven stubble to his bow?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who pursued them, and passed safely By the way that he had not gone with his feet?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I, the Lord, am the first; And with the last I am He.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The coastlands saw it and feared, The ends of the earth were afraid; They drew near and came.
Everyone helped his neighbor,
Be of good courage!
segments.speaker: Neighbors

segments.text: So the craftsman encouraged the goldsmith; He who smooths with the hammer inspired him who strikes the anvil,
It is ready for the soldering
segments.speaker: Craftsman

segments.text: Then he fastened it with pegs, That it might not totter.
But you, Israel, are My servant, Jacob whom I have chosen, The descendants of Abraham My friend.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You whom I have taken from the ends of the earth, And called from its farthest regions, And said to you, 'You are My servant, I have chosen you and have not cast you away:'
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Fear not, for I am with you; Be not dismayed, for I am your God. I will strengthen you, Yes, I will help you, I will uphold you with My righteous right hand.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Behold, all those who were incensed against you Shall be ashamed and disgraced; They shall be as nothing, And those who strive with you shall perish.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall seek them and not find them— Those who contended with you. Those who war against you Shall be as nothing, As a nonexistent thing.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For I, the Lord your God, will hold your right hand, Saying to you, 'Fear not, I will help you.'
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Fear not, you worm Jacob, You men of Israel! I will help you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Behold, I will make you into a new threshing sledge with sharp teeth; You shall thresh the mountains and beat them small, And make the hills like chaff.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall winnow them, the wind shall carry them away, And the whirlwind shall scatter them; You shall rejoice in the Lord, And glory in the Holy One of Israel.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The poor and needy seek water, but there is none, Their tongues fail for thirst. I, the Lord, will hear them; I, the God of Israel, will not forsake them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will open rivers in desolate heights, And fountains in the midst of the valleys; I will make the wilderness a pool of water, And the dry land springs of water.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will plant in the wilderness the cedar and the acacia tree, The myrtle and the oil tree; I will set in the desert the cypress tree and the pine And the box tree together,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: That they may see and know, And consider and understand together, That the hand of the Lord has done this, And the Holy One of Israel has created it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Present your case. Bring forth your strong reasons.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Let them bring forth and show us what will happen; Let them show the former things, what they were, That we may consider them, And know the latter end of them; Or declare to us things to come.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Show the things that are to come hereafter, That we may know that you are gods; Yes, do good or do evil, That we may be dismayed and see it together.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Indeed you are nothing, And your work is nothing; He who chooses you is an abomination.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I have raised up one from the north, And he shall come; From the rising of the sun he shall call on My name; And he shall come against princes as though mortar, As the potter treads clay.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who has declared from the beginning, that we may know? And former times, that we may say, 'He is righteous'? Surely there is no one who shows, Surely there is no one who declares, Surely there is no one who hears your words.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The first time I said to Zion, 'Look, there they are!' And I will give to Jerusalem one who brings good tidings.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For I looked, and there was no man; I looked among them, but there was no counselor, Who, when I asked of them, could answer a word.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Indeed they are all worthless; Their works are nothing; Their molded images are wind and confusion.
segments.speaker: God
